architecture.summary: DRY fix: github-validator.ts delegates to GitHubProvider.validateOwner(). Both implementations capture actual HTTP status from failed fetch responses instead of hardcoding 404. No new types or modules needed — uses existing GitApiError and getActionableError().

integrations.summary: No new integrations. Fix is internal to GitHub API error handling path. git-error-handler.ts already has handle401/handle403/handle404 — just needs the real status passed through. Other providers (GitLab, Bitbucket) out of scope.

ui-ux.summary: No UI changes. Error messages improve: users will see '401 Unauthorized' or '403 Rate Limited' with actionable suggestions instead of misleading '404 Not Found'. This is the existing behavior of git-error-handler.ts that was being bypassed.

performance.summary: No performance impact. The fix changes which status code is passed to an error constructor — same number of API calls, same control flow. No additional network requests.

security.summary: Fix improves security posture: 401 errors now surface auth guidance so users can fix expired/invalid tokens instead of being misled into thinking the owner doesn't exist. No secrets exposed — error messages come from existing handle401/handle403 which are already designed for user-facing output.

edge-cases.summary: Key edge case: user endpoint returns 403 (rate limit) but org endpoint returns 404 (not found). Should prefer the 403 since it's more informative — the 404 may be a side effect of rate limiting. When both return different non-200 statuses, prefer the more severe/informative one (401 > 403 > 404).
